name: FLORENTINE GROTESCHI #20
description: Florentine Groteschi #20 is a glitch candy rot single-panel relic built around Michelangelo and Degen Forum Relic Board. Tarocchi Fiorentini assigns Due di Coppe, used here as the witness card. A corecore chapel chant in broken notation gets pulled into a rarity dashboard fed only by actual trait values, making the panel read like a corrupted receipt for belief, status, and patronage. The guild refuses to explain why the halo has admin rights. The generated insert, a low-res dove dropping governance ballots, acts as the modern clue; Gematria marks DUOMO at Σ 408.

attributes.value: a low-res dove dropping governance ballots

florentine_groteschi.collector_note: Custom top-level lore data: not an OpenSea trait list, but intentionally readable in the raw token JSON.

chess_piece.name: Bishop

rarity.ranking_rule: Higher chess strength/rank is intentionally rarer: Pawn common, Knight/Bishop uncommon, Rook rare, Queen epic, King legendary.

board_position.position_rule: Deterministic per-token board square reserved for future game/use mechanics; independent of visible glyph placement.

chess_piece.allocation_rule: Weighted deterministic shuffled chess allocation by piece strength: {'Pawn': 560, 'Knight': 280, 'Bishop': 280, 'Rook': 168, 'Queen': 84, 'King': 29}
chess_piece.metadata_visibility: Hidden from OpenSea attributes; stored only in florentine_groteschi.hidden_traits.
chess_piece.visual_rule: A small unlabeled foreground chess glyph shows the chess-piece graphic with the piece name encoded underneath; that visible code is intentionally not serialized in metadata.

visible_text.title: FLORENTINE GROTESCHI #20
visible_text.main_caption: Glitch is the cube's way of filing a complaint. Michelangelo is not random dressing here: the Due di Coppe omen matches a stain in the museum security office after midnight, so the guild refuses to explain why the halo has admin rights.
visible_text.history_now_bridge: RELIQUARY SUPPORT TICKET: a corecore chapel chant in broken notation gets pulled into a rarity dashboard fed only by actual trait values. Due di Coppe reads the jump as patronage theater: who certifies the relic, who pays for belief, and who gets the receipt. The guild refuses to explain why the halo has admin rights.
visible_text.period_looksmaxx_plaque: MICHELANGELO MARBLEMAXX MIRROR NOTE: ask a pigeon to audit symmetry while the notary writes what the sigma. The chapel whispers back, witnessed by the candle smoke.
bottom_provenance_strip: MINCHIATE 97×14+43 // DUE DI COPPE // PAL glitch candy rot // BACON RIBBON

artwork_breakdown.generated_insert: a low-res dove dropping governance ballots

tarocchi_fiorentini.allocation_rule: 14 complete 97-card sets across 1,401 tokens, plus 43 deterministic extras

artwork_breakdown.composition_read: Michelangelo anchors the historical source. Due di Coppe supplies the witness logic. The low-res dove dropping governance ballots acts as the modern meme-object. The glitch candy rot palette and Degen Forum Relic Board styling make the panel read like a corrupted Renaissance case file.

groteschi_explanation.plain_english: Read plainly: Michelangelo is treated as evidence in a reliquary support ticket. The old-world clue is a corecore chapel chant in broken notation; the modern-world echo is a rarity dashboard fed only by actual trait values. The joke is that patronage, holy paperwork, and marketplace verification are all status systems asking who gets believed.
groteschi_explanation.read_between_the_lines: The Due di Coppe card is not decorative; it tells you how to read the room. The old clue and the modern clue compete as evidence, so the panel becomes a custody argument about belief, status, and receipts. The guild refuses to explain why the halo has admin rights. The slang turns sacred authority into aura, patronage into clout economics, and marginalia into groupchat evidence.

slang_for_normal_plebs.translation_for_normal_plebs: did it perfectly; in this collection a fresco, saint, or relic can 'eat' like a viral post
ordinary or disappointing, especially when the crowd expected revelation
opponent or enemy; in this world a rival guild, collector, or saintly antagonist
self-mythologized lone-wolf status; a patron, saint, or relic trying too hard to seem untouchable
confused disbelief in groteschi dialect; a marginalia scream when the symbols overperform

hidden_solution.method: Split the visible marker into DNA triplets, translate each triplet through the genetic-code table, then read the amino-acid letters as the source Base name. Non-standard letters use documented stop/nearest triplet placeholders.

hidden_acrostic.method: Read the first character of each acrostic line; slashes are spaces.

collector_warnings: If the Tarocchi card and QR code disagree, trust the card.
If the ledger balances, something has gone terribly wrong.
This file has already been read by one Medici accountant.
Do not cite the notary after midnight.
Due di Coppe is a witness, not a decorative rectangle.
